title: No 1433 for Streamlit Cloud?
content:
OK. I have to post about this.

I have spent 2 days figuring out why my Streamlit code does not connect to SQL Server. The code works fine on local machine and on my DO test droplet.

Always thought that the problem caused by pyodbc but I manage to scope the problem down to port.

Cut to the chase.

Streamlit Cloud does not like 1433, the standard SQL Server port. Add a new listening port and the code just work. There is not mention of this in the documentation.

I might be wrong, but it works for me so there's that.
